MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-23-2022 Off Calendar

Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or, In The Alternative An Order Compelling Plaintiffs To Produce Documents And Pay Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 5,900 (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Off Calendar

Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or, In The Alternative An Order Compelling Plaintiffs To Produce Documents And Pay Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 5,900; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS BRIAN SORIANO and LAW OFFICE OF BRIAN E. SORIANO's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or, In The Alternative An Order Compelling Plaintiffs To Produce Documents And Pay Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $5,900.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion for terminating sanctions is DENIED but the motion to compel and for sanctions is GRANTED. Defendant has shown that Plaintiffs have not fully complied with their discovery obligations or Court orders. There is still no verified statement that production has concluded. Therefore, the request for an order requiring complete production of all outstanding documents is GRANTED. Plaintiffs must serve all outstanding documents no later than March 25, 2022. Plaintiffs also must either serve an updated verified discovery response that states that Plaintiffs complied with the Code and their representations they would produce all documents not logged or submit a declaration that sets out the steps taken to search for potentially responsive documents. The request for terminating sanctions is DENIED because such sanctions would put the party entitled to but denied discovery in a better position than if they received discovery. CCP 2023.030; Lopez v. Watchtower Bible & Tract Soc. (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 566, 604-05. In making this finding I consider that, in a legal malpractice case like this, defendant reasonably can be expected to have many of the records requested. While this does not relieve Plaintiffs of the obligation to produce records within their possession, custody, or control, it militates against terminating sanctions. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. The motion is granted in part and denied in part.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Motion To Compel Plaintiff Joy Holiday Inc.S Further Responses To Requests For Production And Production Of Documents And Monetary Sanctions (Motions)
Off Calendar Mar-09-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. WENDY M. THOMAS, Esq. of Tadjedin Thomas & Engbloom Law Group LLP (Tel: 310-454-5900) for defendants/cross-complainants Brian E. Soriano and Law Office of Brian E. Soriano;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS BRIAN SORIANO AND LAW OFFICE OF BRIAN E. SORIANO's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Joy Holiday Inc.'s Further Responses To Requests For Production And Production Of Documents And Monetary Sanctions.

Defendants stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for defendants (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Mr. Soriano's motion is partially GRANTED as follows:

Good cause appearing, Joy Holiday's objections to document requests Nos. 1 - 3, 5 - 7, 16 - 19, and 22 - 27 are overruled. Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 2025.450(a), (b)(1). It shall produce all responsive documents within its possession, custody or control by March 30, 2022 that it has not already produced to Mr. Soriano. It shall likewise produce all documents responsive to requests Nos. 8 - 10, 12, 13 and 15 subject to the same requirements. If Joy Holiday withholds documents on grounds of privilege or privacy, it shall provide a log, including the specific grounds of each claim of privilege or privacy.

Joy Holiday's objections to document requests Nos. 20 & 21 are sustained at this time.
Mr. Soriano's request for monetary sanctions is conditionally GRANTED as follows: Joy Holiday and its counsel of record are ordered to pay $4,000 in monetary sanctions to Mr. Soriano if it does not timely comply with this order. Code Civ. Proc. Secs. 2023.010, 2023.030(a), 2025.450(g)(1).

Counsel for defendants is instructed to prepare and submit a proposed order in Word format to the pro tem judge by email sent to pvestal@nvlawllp.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
